text: Optical neural networks (ONNs) offer a promising pathway toward high-speed, energy-efficient artificial intelligence hardware by leveraging the massive parallelism of light. However, scaling these architectures while achieving high-level integration and operational precision remains a significant challenge. In this work, we demonstrate a fully integrated photonic processing unit (PPU) driven by an on-chip AlGaAs-on-insulator dark-pulse microcomb. By implementing a convolution scheme based on time-wavelength plane stretching, we enable high-speed parallel data processing within a compact footprint. To overcome the sensitivities inherent in integrated analog photonics, we developed a specialized control and calibration protocol, achieving a record weight precision of 9 bits. The resulting system exhibits a preeminent photonic-core compute density of 1.04 trillion operations per second per square millimeter (TOPS mm⁻²), outperforming previous wavelength-division multiplexing (WDM) ONN architectures by a factor of five. We validate the PPU through proof-of-concept demonstrations of image edge detection and handwritten digit recognition, achieving classification accuracies comparable to digital computers. This highly scalable approach marks a transition for parallel photonic accelerators from laboratory prototypes toward practical, high-performance deployment in fields such as autonomous driving and real-time video analysis.
